name: Timer CHOP
description: The Timer CHOP is an engine for running timed processes. It outputs channels such as timing fractions, counters, pulses and timer states, and it calls python functions (callbacks) when various timing events occur.
summary: The Timer CHOP is an engine for running timed processes. It outputs channels such as timing fractions, counters, pulses and timer states, and it calls python functions (callbacks) when various timing

parameters.name: Active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: The Timer cooks: Never / Always / While Running (while in "running" state) / While Playing (while in "running" state and Play is on).

parameters.name: Never
parameters.label: Never
parameters.description: never - always - running - playing -

parameters.name: Time Control
parameters.label: Time Control
parameters.description:
timecontrol -  - Sequential (timeline-independent) or Locked to . In Locked to , non-deterministic features are disabled. External   lets you drive the master time (.masterSeconds etc) using a  channel defined by the parameters on the External page. External Timecode lets you drive the master time using a timecode //.

 lock - Output is determined by the current frame position. sequential - Output runs continuously, regardless of current frame position. chop - Output is determined by external  channel value. timecode - Output is determined by the value of an external timecode.

parameters.name: Lock To Timeline
parameters.label: Lock To Timeline
parameters.description: lock - Output is determined by the current frame position. sequential - Output runs continuously, regardless of current frame position. chop - Output is determined by external  channel value. timecode - Output is determined by the value of an external timecode.

parameters.name: Defer Par Changes
parameters.label: Defer Par Changes
parameters.description: deferpars - When On, parameter changes like Length, Cycles and others are ignored until the next Intitalize. When Off, paraameter changes affect the timer immediately (possibly giving jumps in state).

parameters.name: Initialize
parameters.label: Initialize
parameters.description: initialize - (pulse parameter) Initialize is the signal to get the timer ready: sets the counters to zero (delay, timer, cycle, segment), set the output channels in the proper state, done to be off, the onInitialize() callback is run, and when initialize is complete, it indicates it’s ready by turning on the ready channel, awaiting a Start pulse.

parameters.name: Start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: start - (pulse) Start is the signal to commence the timers counting. It will count through the delay first, then the timer length. It does an Initialize if it is not already initialized, and then starts counting.

parameters.name: Length Type
parameters.label: Length Type
parameters.description:
lengthtype -  - Describes how the length is defined.

 fixed - Lengths are finite, that is of a specific duration. infinite - The segments run continuously without end.

parameters.name: Fixed
parameters.label: Fixed
parameters.description: fixed - Lengths are finite, that is of a specific duration. infinite - The segments run continuously without end.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.label: Length
parameters.description: length - (float) the time-length of the timer. Set the Units menu to Seconds, Frames or Samples.

parameters.name: Length Units
parameters.label: Length Units
parameters.description:
lengthunits -  - Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

 samples - frames - seconds -

parameters.name: I
parameters.label: I
parameters.description: samples - frames - seconds -

parameters.name: Delay
parameters.label: Delay
parameters.description: delay - (float) after Start, the delay before the timer begins counting.

parameters.name: Delay Units
parameters.label: Delay Units
parameters.description:
delayunits -  - Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

 samples - frames - seconds -

parameters.name: Play
parameters.label: Play
parameters.description: play - (onoff) Pauses the timer. It is basically a 0 or 1 multiplier on the Speed.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.label: Speed
parameters.description: speed - (default 1) Slows down or speeds up the timer.

parameters.name: Cue
parameters.label: Cue
parameters.description: cue - Freezes playing at the Cue .

parameters.name: Cue Pulse
parameters.label: Cue Pulse
parameters.description: cuepulse - Jump instantly to the Cue .

parameters.name: Cue Point
parameters.label: Cue Point
parameters.description: cuepoint - Time (Seconds, Frames or Fraction) which the cue point is frozen to.

parameters.name: Cue Units
parameters.label: Cue Units
parameters.description:
cueunits -  - Choose between using Samples, Frames, Seconds, Fraction(0-1) as the units for this parameter.

 samples - frames - seconds - fraction -

parameters.name: Cycle
parameters.label: Cycle
parameters.description: cycle - (default Off) causes the timer to loop back to 0 when it reaches the end of the cycle.

parameters.name: Cycle Limit
parameters.label: Cycle Limit
parameters.description: cyclelimit - When the Cycle parameter is On, this determines if it will cycle indefinitely or cycle some maximum number of cycles.

parameters.name: Maximum Cycles
parameters.label: Maximum Cycles
parameters.description: maxcycles - When Cycle is on and Cycle Limit is on, this sets the maximum number of cycles.

parameters.name: Cycle End Alert
parameters.label: Cycle End Alert
parameters.description: cycleendalert - The number of seconds, frames or samples before a cycle, segment or done state is reached that the onCycleEndAlert() callback is called. This allows you to prepare for the next cycle, segment or timer.

parameters.name: Notify Units
parameters.label: Notify Units
parameters.description:
notifyunits -  - Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

 samples - frames - seconds -

parameters.name: Exit Segment at End of Cycle
parameters.label: Exit Segment at End of Cycle
parameters.description: exitendcycle - When pulsed, it will exit the cycle (and segment) at the end of the currently-playing cycle.

parameters.name: Go to End of Cycle
parameters.label: Go to End of Cycle
parameters.description: gotoendcycle - When pulsed, it will exit the cycle (and segment) immediately.

parameters.name: Go to Done
parameters.label: Go to Done
parameters.description: gotodone - Will immediately go to the Done state.

parameters.name: On Done
parameters.label: On Done
parameters.description:
ondone -  - Determines which action to take when the timer gets to the end, ie "is done" or finished. Note there is also a onDone callback that can be used for customizing behavior.

 donothing - No action taken. reinit - Will re-initialize the timer, this is the same as pulsing the Initialize parameter above. restart - Will re-initialize and re-start the timer, this is the same as pulsing the Start parameter above. restartnoinit - Will re-star the timeer without re-initializing.

parameters.name: Do Nothing
parameters.label: Do Nothing
parameters.description: donothing - No action taken. reinit - Will re-initialize the timer, this is the same as pulsing the Initialize parameter above. restart - Will re-initialize and re-start the timer, this is the same as pulsing the Start parameter above. restartnoinit - Will re-star the timeer without re-initializing.

parameters.name: Callbacks DAT
parameters.label: Callbacks DAT
parameters.description: callbacks - The path to the  containing callbacks for this Timer .

parameters.name: Segments DAT
parameters.label: Segments DAT
parameters.description:
segdat - A table  that contains one row per timer (segment). The column headings can be delay or begin, length, cycle, cyclelimit, maxcycles and cycleendalert, which override the equivalent parameters. (These are the internal names for the corresponding parameters.) begin is unique as it replaces delay, and it represents the time from Start that the timer will begin counting, whether the  is set to Serial Timers or Parallel Timers (see Segment Method). 	
The Segments  also can include any number of custom columns. See Columns to Custom Channels and Columns to Info  below.

parameters.name: Segment Method
parameters.label: Segment Method
parameters.description:
segmethod -  - If the Segment Method is Serial Timers, the timers will be played back-to-back. If the Segment Method is Parallel Timers, the timers can be played at the same time, and a set of channels will be output for each timer.

 serial - parallel -

parameters.name: Serial Timers
parameters.label: Serial Timers
parameters.description: serial - parallel -

parameters.name: Segment Units
parameters.label: Segment Units
parameters.description:
segunits -  - For the columns delay, begin, length and cycleendalert, you specify whether it’s seconds, frames or samples with this menu.

 samples - frames - seconds -

parameters.name: Samples
parameters.label: Samples
parameters.description: samples - frames - seconds -

parameters.name: Segments End Time
parameters.label: Segments End Time
parameters.description:
segsendtime -  - Describes how the end time is calculated.

 dat - The end time is based on the Segments  values. par - The end time is based on the Length parameter value. max - The end time is based on the greater of the Length parameter value and the Segments  values.

parameters.name: From Segments DAT
parameters.label: From Segments DAT
parameters.description: dat - The end time is based on the Segments  values. par - The end time is based on the Length parameter value. max - The end time is based on the greater of the Length parameter value and the Segments  values.

parameters.name: Columns to Custom Channels
parameters.label: Columns to Custom Channels
parameters.description: channelcolumns - Optional extra columns (any name) in the segments  can be output as extra channels (the columns must contain numbers). Specify their names in the Columns to Channels parameter. The channel name will be the column name. You can also output the length, delay, etc columns as channels.

parameters.name: Custom Channel Interpolation
parameters.label: Custom Channel Interpolation
parameters.description:
interpolation -  - By default, custom channels step to their new value at the begin of the segment. This menu lets you interpolate to the new value linearly, or any combination of ease-in and ease-out.

 steptovalue - lineartovalue - easeintovalue - easeouttovalue - easeinouttovalue -

parameters.name: Step to Value
parameters.label: Step to Value
parameters.description: steptovalue - lineartovalue - easeintovalue - easeouttovalue - easeinouttovalue -

parameters.name: Columns to Info DAT
parameters.label: Columns to Info DAT
parameters.description: infocolumns - Optional extra columns (any name) in the segments  can be output to the Info  (attach an Info  to the Timer ) if you specify their names in this parameter.

parameters.name: Go to Previous Segment
parameters.label: Go to Previous Segment
parameters.description: gotoprevseg - (pulse) Jump to Previous Segment.

parameters.name: Go to Next Segment
parameters.label: Go to Next Segment
parameters.description:
gotonextseg - (pulse) Jump to Next Segment.	
Lingo			

Segment – each segment acts as one timer, with delay time, length, number of cycles to repeat and other conditions.
Begin – in Parallel Timers, the number of seconds after a Start (frames or samples) after which each timer starts counting up from zero.
Done – The state it goes into when all the timers has finished counting, whether they are in Parallel or Serial, Segments or not.
End – Cycle End is the end of each cycle, Segment End is the end of the segment.
Cumulative Time – Zero at Start, a count that is affected by speed and rises while timers are active (not during delays).
Running Time – Zero at Start, the wall-clock time since Start was called no matter what are the delays, speeds, cycles or premature clicking of Go To Segment End. It stops counting when Done has been reached.

parameters.name: Sub Range
parameters.label: Sub Range
parameters.description: subrange - Turn this parameter on to limit the timer output to a subrange of the full length.

parameters.name: Sub Start
parameters.label: Sub Start
parameters.description: substart - The beginning point of the sub range.

parameters.name: Sub Start Units
parameters.label: Sub Start Units
parameters.description:
substartunits -  - Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

 samples - frames - seconds -

parameters.name: Sub End
parameters.label: Sub End
parameters.description: subend - The end point of the sub range.

parameters.name: Sub End Units
parameters.label: Sub End Units
parameters.description:
subendunits -  - Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

 samples - frames - seconds -

parameters.name: Sub End Action
parameters.label: Sub End Action
parameters.description:
subendaction -  - Controls the behavior once the sub range end point is reached: Loop at End, or Pause at End.

 pause - loop -

parameters.name: Pause at End
parameters.label: Pause at End
parameters.description: pause - loop -

parameters.name: Timer Fraction
parameters.label: Timer Fraction
parameters.description: outfraction - Outputs channel timer_fraction for each segment.

parameters.name: Timer Count
parameters.label: Timer Count
parameters.description:
outtimercount -  - Outputs the elapsed Seconds channel as timer_seconds, Frames outputs channel as timer_frames, or Samples outputs channel as timer_samples. Because this is elapsed time, timer_frames starts at 0, as do the others.

 off - samples - frames - seconds - all -

parameters.name: Off
parameters.label: Off
parameters.description: off - samples - frames - seconds - all -

parameters.name: Timer Active
parameters.label: Timer Active
parameters.description: outtimeractive - Outputs channel timer_active which is on only while the timer fraction is counting (is non-zero).

parameters.name: Timer Pulse
parameters.label: Timer Pulse
parameters.description: outtimerpulse - Outputs channel timer_pulse when the timer reaches its length.

parameters.name: Delay Fraction
parameters.label: Delay Fraction
parameters.description: outdelayfraction - Outputs a 0-1 fraction in delay_fraction while the delay occurs.

parameters.name: Delay Count
parameters.label: Delay Count
parameters.description:
outdelaycount -  - Outputs the delay count in seconds, frames or samples.

 off - samples - frames - seconds - all -

parameters.name: Initializing
parameters.label: Initializing
parameters.description: outinit - Outputs channel initializing = 1 while the timer is initalizing (i.e. while the callback onInitialize() returns non-zero).

parameters.name: Ready
parameters.label: Ready
parameters.description: outready - Outputs channel ready which is 1 after an Initialize and before a Start.

parameters.name: Ready Pulse
parameters.label: Ready Pulse
parameters.description: outreadypulse - Outputs a pulse when initialization has finished and the timer is ready to start. It pulses even when the timer starts rights away after an initialization.

parameters.name: Running
parameters.label: Running
parameters.description: outrunning - Outputs channel running which is 1 after a Start and before the Done.

parameters.name: Done
parameters.label: Done
parameters.description: outdone - Outputs channel done when done or complete.

parameters.name: Done Pulse
parameters.label: Done Pulse
parameters.description: outdonepulse - Outputs channel done when the all timers have reached their completion.

parameters.name: Cycles
parameters.label: Cycles
parameters.description: outcycle - Outputs channel cycles, which is the number of cycles completed (In a segment), starting with 0 during the entire first cycle. If you jump to Done, cycle is incremented as if it played normally to the done state.

parameters.name: Cycle Pulse
parameters.label: Cycle Pulse
parameters.description: outcyclepulse - Outputs a pulse at the end of every cycle, even on the first and only cycle.

parameters.name: Cycles + Fraction
parameters.label: Cycles + Fraction
parameters.description: outcycleplusfraction - Outputs channel cycle_plus_fraction, starting with 0 for entire first cycle.

parameters.name: Segment
parameters.label: Segment
parameters.description: outseg - Outputs channel segment, starting with 0 for first segment.

parameters.name: Segment Pulse
parameters.label: Segment Pulse
parameters.description: outsegpulse - Outputs channel segment_pulse which is a pulse at the end of each segment.

parameters.name: Segment + Fraction
parameters.label: Segment + Fraction
parameters.description: outsegplusfraction - Outputs channel segment_plus_fraction, starting with 0 for first segment ending at #segments at end.

parameters.name: Cumulative Timer Count
parameters.label: Cumulative Timer Count
parameters.description:
outcumulativecount -  - Outputs cumulative_seconds, cumulative_frames or cumulative_samples. It is a time count that adds up all the Timer Active times for all segments since Start: it is affected by "Speed", and counts up only while timer_active (Play) is on.   See the python member .cumulativeSeconds.

 off - samples - frames - seconds - all -

parameters.name: Playing Timer Count
parameters.label: Playing Timer Count
parameters.description:
outplayingcount -  - Outputs playing_seconds, playing_frames or playing_samples. It is a time count that adds up all the Timer Active times for all segments since Start: it is not affected by "Speed", and counts up only while timer_active and play is on.  See the python member .playingSeconds.

 off - seconds - frames - samples - all -

parameters.name: Running Time Count
parameters.label: Running Time Count
parameters.description:
outrunningcount -  - Outputs the "wall-clock" time since Start occurred, no matter what are the delays, speeds, cycles or pre-mature clicking of Go To Segment End, etc. It stops counting when Done has been reached. running_seconds, running_frames, or running_samples. When  is set to Parallel Timers, this will output a channel per segment plus one global running time channel.   See the python member .runningSeconds.

 off - samples - frames - seconds - all -

parameters.name: Master Time Count
parameters.label: Master Time Count
parameters.description:
outmastercount -  - Outputs master_seconds, master_frames or master_samples. It is a time count that adds up all the Timer Active times for all segments since Start: it is affected by "Speed", and counts up only while timer_active and play is on. It also includes any delay times.   See the python member .masterSeconds.

 off - seconds - frames - samples - all -

parameters.name: External CHOP
parameters.label: External CHOP
parameters.description: extchop - The  used to control the current point in the timer.

parameters.name: External Units
parameters.label: External Units
parameters.description:
extunits -  - Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

 samples - frames - seconds - fraction -

parameters.name: External Channel
parameters.label: External Channel
parameters.description: extchannel - The channel that will control the current point of the timer.

parameters.name: External Timecode Object
parameters.label: External Timecode Object
parameters.description: exttcobj - Time is specified using a timecode. Should be a reference to either a  with channels 'hour', 'second', 'minute', 'frame', a  with a timecode string in its first cell, or a Timecode Class object.

parameters.name: External Start Offset
parameters.label: External Start Offset
parameters.description: extstartoff - Specifies the external time at which the timer should start.

parameters.name: External Initialize Offset
parameters.label: External Initialize Offset
parameters.description: extinitoff - Specifies the external time at which the timer should initialize.

parameters.name: Sample Rate
parameters.label: Sample Rate
parameters.description: rate - The sample rate that the  outputs at, which is also used when the units of Length, Delay and Cycle End Alert time are set to Samples. The default sample rate is 60 samples per second.

parameters.name: Time Slice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: timeslice - Turning this on forces the channels to be "Time Sliced".  A  is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: Scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: scope - To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a  string on the Common page.

parameters.name: Sample Rate Match
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description:
srselect -  - Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Options are not available.

 first - Use rate of first input to resample others. max - Resample to the highest sample rate. min - Resample to the lowest sample rate. err - Doesn't accept conflicting sample rates.

parameters.name: Resample At First Input's Rate
parameters.label: Resample At First Input's Rate
parameters.description: first - Use rate of first input to resample others. max - Resample to the highest sample rate. min - Resample to the lowest sample rate. err - Doesn't accept conflicting sample rates.

parameters.name: Export Method
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description:
exportmethod -  - This will determine how to connect the  channel to the parameter. Refer to the Export article for more information.

 datindex - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the index of the channel in the . datname - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the name of the channel in the . autoname - The channel is the full destination of where to export to, such has geo1/transform1:tx.

parameters.name: DAT Table by Index
parameters.label: DAT Table by Index
parameters.description: datindex - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the index of the channel in the . datname - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the name of the channel in the . autoname - The channel is the full destination of where to export to, such has geo1/transform1:tx.

parameters.name: Export Root
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: autoexportroot - This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by Channel Name is : are relative to.

parameters.name: Export Table
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: exporttable - The  used to hold the export information when using the  Table  Methods (See above).
